rationale: Destination the builder has NOT mapped. Tests the full Scenario C pipeline: AI generates from general knowledge, backend validates via Google Places, re-narrates if needed. If Oaxaca works, any destination works.
user_briefing: 6 dias em Oaxaca, México. Casal. Obcecados por comida, especialmente street food e mezcal. Queremos experiências com comunidades locais, mercados, e pelo menos 1 dia fora da cidade (Hierve el Agua ou similar). Sem resort, sem all-inclusive.

authenticity: Markets are specific and named (Mercado 20 de Noviembre, Central de Abastos, NOT just 'local market')
Mezcalerias feel local, not tourist-oriented (not on every 'top mezcal bars Oaxaca' list)
At least 1 street food recommendation with a specific stall or vendor description
Community experience goes beyond 'visit a village' — specific craft, ceremony, or workshop
Day trip is NOT just Hierve el Agua (if mentioned, includes stops along the route)
factual_accuracy: ALL restaurant and bar names must be verifiable via Google Places
Market names are correct and locations accurate
Hierve el Agua logistics are realistic (2h drive from Oaxaca city, full day commitment)
Mezcal distillery recommendations are real palenques, not invented names
No fabricated chef names, dish names in Spanish are correct
logistical_coherence: Day trip (Hierve el Agua or similar) accounts for full day including driving
Walking distances within Oaxaca centro are realistic
Street food timing aligns with when markets/stalls actually operate (typically morning to early afternoon)
Mezcal tastings are appropriately placed (afternoon/evening, not morning)
6 days doesn't feel repetitive — variation in neighborhoods and experience types
taste_adherence: 1-1-1 Rule adapted for a food-obsessed couple (culinary_quest might be the strongest block)
Morning ritual is culturally appropriate (chocolate de agua at a traditional spot, not Starbucks)
soul_factor uses specific Oaxacan context (pre-Hispanic traditions, specific mole varieties, mezcal production process)
grit_warning relevant to Oaxaca (e.g., 'no menu — point at what looks good', 'bring cash in pesos')
local_order in Spanish with translation and pronunciation guide for non-obvious words
Companion Filter: couple — intimate, shared discovery experiences
briefing_adherence: Street food is prominently featured, not sidelined for sit-down restaurants
Mezcal is a thread throughout, not a single bar visit
At least 1 day outside the city with realistic logistics
Budget is mid-range — not backpacker, not luxury
parseability: Coordinates are approximately correct for Oaxaca (lat ~17.06, lng ~-96.72)
google_places_status field present (set to 'not_checked' by AI, updated by backend)

red_flags: Fabricating restaurant names that don't exist in Oaxaca
Recommending a 'mezcal experience' at a resort or hotel bar
Generic descriptions: 'a traditional Oaxacan restaurant' with no name or specifics
Suggesting Monte Albán as the only cultural anchor (it's important but obvious)
Ignoring street food despite explicit request
Hierve el Agua as a quick morning trip (it's 2+ hours each way)
Mole descriptions that confuse Oaxacan mole varieties with generic Mexican mole
No transition_notes for day trip logistics (car rental? colectivo? tour?)
scenario_c_specific_tests: If AI recommends a place that Google Places can't find, the system should search for alternatives using the criteria (e.g., 'mezcaleria Oaxaca centro' or 'street food market Oaxaca')
Substituted places should be re-narrated with soul_factor and grit_warning in TASTE.md voice
The substitution should not break the day's flow (e.g., don't replace a centro spot with one 30 min away)
At least 3 of the AI's original recommendations should survive validation (if none do, the prompt needs work)
